So, we're going to be speaking about the prohibition of plucking white or gray hair. And furthermore, the encouragement of the prophet sallall.aii am to instead of plucking the gray hair rather to use dye or use to change the color Muslim >> >> on the authority of who narrates from his father who narrated from his grandfather that the messenger of Allah wasallam said do not do not pluck your gray hairs. There is no Muslim that will grow a gray or white hair in Islam except that that white hair will act as a source of light on the day of judgment. Why? Well, because this is very similar to the stripes that a veteran has. And a person is Muslim and they endure and they hold on to Islam and they remain steadfast on the path of Allah until they start to grow old because traditionally gray hair, white hair is is a you know is a hallmark of of age, right? then this is a testament to this person's to this person's steadfastness on Islam. And so it becomes a source of light for them on the day of judgment. So if we're not to pluck these gray hairs, then what are we to do? The author says, for. So in the time of the prophet sallall.aii wasallam they had different ways of dying or changing the hair color. They had what is called that which is is well known. We call it in English ha and al ketam. Al ketam is actually a type of plant that when mixed with a type of tree known as elesma. They used to mix these two together and it would form a dye. And this dye is a little bit deeper and darker than a hat. And it is likewise very it will leave a kind of stain on the skin similar to alah right. It will wash off after enough time but it was used in his time saiam on hair and it was used also on hands and you know women would use it as well for beautifification. The author then says and anything of the sort. So is it a condition that a person use specifically or that a person use specifically ketam? No, not necessarily. However, the only thing that is prohibited is black. Black dye. Dying the hair black is not just disliked. It is it is haram in the correct opinion. In the fourth Abu D narrates that the messenger of Allah wasallam said and encouraged and advised that the best thing that you would change your white hairs with or dye your white hairs with is which we described. for a shape. Well, he says and the the question then arises is that how do we harmonize between the narration that we heard just a few minutes ago and this narration. If white hair will that a person grows or develops in Islam will act as a source of light on the day of judgment. Then why would the prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam advised that it be changed that it be dyed that it be colored and the answer to this is in a narration in Bkari Muslim on the authority of in which the prophet sallallahu alaii wasallam says that the Jews and the Christians do not dye their hair and dye their white hair or their gray hair. So so you know meaning so you know contradict them right contradict them. Now what does this mean? The Jews and the Christians. This these are huge communities. Does this mean that not a single Jew everywhere anywhere in any time or a single Christian anywhere or any time will you know use dye in in his or her hair? No, that's not what's intended. What's intended is they do not do it out of religiosity. They do not religiously dye their white hairs. So the prophet sallallah.aii wasallam tells us to differentiate ourselves from the Jews and the Christians in our religious practices. And this is very important. This is a general this is a general principle in Islam and that is that the legislation of Islam goes out of its way to differentiate the Muslim in generally and specific acts of worship from the Jews or the Christians or any other religious group for that matter. It is a part of Islam and its practice that a person deliberately differentiate himself from all other groups. Now why is that? This is such a principle that Islam he wrote an entire book explaining this principle and he named the book. He said part of the straight path of Allah is differentiating oneself from the people of hell. To the point that the prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam chose to modify the days that we fast to modify the way that we fast to modify the way that we speak to modify the way that we walk to modify the clothes that we wear. Not everything they do is wrong, right? There's some correct things that they do. And so we don't just do things that are correct. If it is correct, we don't throw it out. But Islam modifies it. So how is it modified? We fast ash but we fast another day with it. So we fast two days. Why? To differentiate ourselves from the Jews. Not only that but the fasting itself is modified because the prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam tells us that the Jews fast on into on in past sunset. They p they fast past sunset until the stars you know become obvious and numerous in the sky, right? Not just the bright ones are visible. So the night sky and the stars are very visible. So what are we to do when we fast? As soon as the disc of the sun is no longer visible behind the horizon, we break our fast, right? and so forth. So the only thing you would see is the braids, three and sometimes he would have four braids and that was the way that you know men kept their hair. Men men men always had long hair. was viewed as being you know a a symbol of masculinity and strength etc. And so when he came to Medina and he saw that the Jews of Medina also kept their hair that, what did he start doing? Part it right down the middle. He would part it right down the middle and then braid it. Why? Well, his head is covered all the time in public. No one would know the difference, right? He knows the difference inside. He does not want to be observed by Allah even superficially appearing like those of disbelief, those of other religions, right? summary: This lecture discusses the Islamic ruling on gray and white hair, specifically prohibiting plucking them while encouraging dyeing with colors other than black. The speaker explains that white hair grown in Islam serves as a light on the Day of Judgment, similar to the stripes of honor on a warrior. The lecture cites hadith from Abu Dawud and other sources, detailing permissible dyes like henna and katam that were used in the Prophet's time. The speaker emphasizes a broader Islamic principle of differentiating Muslims from other religious groups in practices and appearance, providing examples such as fasting practices, hairstyles, and prayer conduct to illustrate this concept.
suggested_title: The Islamic Ruling on Dyeing Gray Hair and the Principle of Differentiation from Other Religions
hadith_chapter: Book of Purification - Chapter on Not Plucking Gray Hair
